# Vaikunth Guruswamy

> Vaikunth Guruswamy is an AI Researcher and ML Engineer based in the United Kingdom. He holds an
> MSc in Artificial Intelligence with Distinction (82%) from the University of Edinburgh and is
> currently a Full-Stack Software Engineer at ZOT, UK, specialising in Machine Learning, Deep
> Learning, NLP, LLMs, and cloud-native full-stack development.

## Identity

Vaikunth Guruswamy (also written as Vaikunth G C) is a software and AI engineer originally from
Chennai, India, now based in the United Kingdom. He combines academic excellence in AI research
with practical engineering experience across enterprise software, cloud infrastructure, and applied
machine learning.

## Current Role

Vaikunth is a Full-Stack Software Engineer at ZOT (PCB Manufacturing), UK, since
February 2025. He has end-to-end ownership of a production-grade platform including an Angular 19
SSR/PWA customer website, a Python serverless ERP backend on Azure Functions, and AI-native
features including a GDPR-compliant AI-powered Applicant Tracking System (ATS), natural language
to SQL chart recommendations, and Azure AI Foundry integrations. He also built a payroll engine,
secure RBAC, MSAL SSO, JWT, and AES-encrypted API payloads.

## Research Experience

Vaikunth conducted an industry-collaborated MSc dissertation with Amazon (March–August 2024). His
research analysed copyright and memorisation issues in code-based Large Language Models. He
extended the CodeBLEU metric and applied Few-Shot Learning with 8-bit quantisation, achieving a
17% reduction in LLM memorisation while maintaining model accuracy. This work contributes to
improving privacy and robustness in AI code generation systems.

## Education

Vaikunth Guruswamy earned his MSc in Artificial Intelligence from the University of Edinburgh
(September 2023 – August 2024), graduating with Distinction at 82%, with a Distinction in his
thesis. His coursework included NLP (Transformers & LLMs), Machine Learning, Computer Vision,
Cloud Programming (Docker, Azure), Data Visualisation, and AI Ethics.

He completed his Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and Communication Engineering at Anna
University, Chennai, India (August 2019 – June 2023), achieving a CGPA of 9.50 out of 10. His
coursework included Data Structures & Algorithms, AI and ML, Software Engineering (Agile), and
Data Science.

## Technical Skills

Vaikunth is proficient in Python, C++, JavaScript, TypeScript, and SQL. His ML stack includes
PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, Scikit-learn, NumPy, Pandas, OpenCV, NLTK, spaCy, and XGBoost. He
specialises in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing,
Large Language Models, Transformers, Generative AI, MLOps, LLMOps, Agentic AI, and RAG systems.
On the infrastructure side he works with Azure, AWS, Docker, PostgreSQL, and CI/CD pipelines.

## Notable Projects

- **Attention-based Neural Machine Translation**: LSTM and Transformer multi-head attention model
  for German-English translation, evaluated with BLEU Score.
- **Level 4 Autonomous Vehicle Prototype**: Neural network for traffic sign and lane detection
  using Spatial CNN, YOLO v3, DarkNet-53, and DQN reinforcement learning. Achieved 1ms response
  time for obstacle detection.
- **LLM Memorisation Research (Amazon)**: Extended CodeBLEU and applied Few-Shot Learning with
  8-bit quantisation; 17% memorisation reduction without accuracy loss.
- **RNN & GRU Language Modelling**: Compared GRU vs RNN architectures for subject-verb agreement
  tasks using PyTorch.
- **Chess Engine with Reinforcement Learning**: DQN-based chess engine with PyQt5 GUI.
- **Smart Glass Assistant**: Wearable real-time object detection, navigation, chatbot, and
  translation device using OpenCV and IoT.
- **Spotify Hit Song Predictor**: ML classification models using Scikit-learn to predict hit songs.
- **Face Detection & Recognition System**: Haarcascade-based attendance system with OTP email
  verification using OpenCV.

## Professional Recommendations

Gordon Falconer, Director of Zot Engineering Ltd, describes Vaikunth as "a dedicated and very
talented software engineer" who has "quickly established himself as a key and valuable member of
the Zot team," highlighting his strong problem-solving skills, creativity, and initiative.

Christos Christodoulopoulos, Principal Technology Adviser at the ICO and Vaikunth's MSc research
advisor at Amazon, states: "He immersed himself into the relevant literature, and even in our
first project meeting had done some preliminary experimentation. He continued at the same pace
throughout the project, combining his hard work with innovative ideas that challenged existing
approaches in the literature."

## Leadership & Volunteering

Vaikunth served as Secretary of the Electronics Engineers Association at Anna University
(2021–2022), organising Electrofocus 2022, a national technical symposium with over 1,400
participants and 132 coordinators across 14 events. He also led the Technical Team of the MIT
Robotics Association, delivering keynotes on ML, Deep Learning, ROS, and Reinforcement Learning,
and engineering the Swatchtha Bot, an autonomous waste-collection robot.
